danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprite entities with distinct visual design (flying enemies, walking guards)
danger_signals.meaning: Hazards to avoid; collision causes damage or life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen change to specialized room layout with countdown display
danger_signals.meaning: Punishment state triggered by greedy resource collection (>9 Mighty Coins or >99 seconds); player must complete jump challenges to escape

danger_signals.visual_form: Chest sprites without visual indication of contents or access state
danger_signals.meaning: Some chests locked behind color-based Mighty Coin requirements; inaccessible chests signal resource scarcity

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen countdown timer visible in action zones
danger_signals.meaning: Temporal pressure; running out of time triggers level failure

danger_signals.visual_form: End-level antagonist sprite (visual design not detailed in sources)
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate danger; must be defeated to complete game objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden in treasure chests, consumable item sprite
safety_signals.meaning: Adds 10 seconds to timer; extends survival window

safety_signals.visual_form: Jack changes to blue color after collecting blue Mighty Coin
safety_signals.meaning: Grants access to orange treasure chests; unlocks hidden resources

safety_signals.visual_form: Jack changes to orange color after collecting orange Mighty Coin
safety_signals.meaning: Allows opening any treasure chest by touching from side; unrestricted resource access

safety_signals.visual_form: Jack changes to green color after collecting green Mighty Coin
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability; transforms all on-screen enemies into coins for 5 seconds

safety_signals.visual_form: Coin/currency sprite items within treasure chests
Revealed by finding Sphinx trigger in hidden or visible treasure chest
safety_signals.meaning: Shortcuts and hidden progression routes; allow level advancement without full completion

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Royal Palace rooms serve as half of each level; distinct visual aesthetic from action zones. Palace rooms borrowed from original Bomb Jack arcade mechanics.

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Flying enemies with distinct sprite designs; unused walking guards and wall hatches suggest planned enemy variety
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: 16-level pyramid structure represents demon's domain; holds royal family hostage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Color-state variations indicate power-up acquisition; visual transformation signals capability shift
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in both Action Zones and Royal Palace rooms; navigates dual-environment level structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct tileset featuring platforms, treasure chests, hidden passages; separate visual theme from Palace
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exploration and collection-focused gameplay; enemies patrol; multiple portions/segments allow route variation

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Royal Palace Room (second half of level)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Palace-themed tileset with architectural elements; direct visual lift from original Bomb Jack arcade
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Collection/achievement focused; mechanics replicate arcade predecessor; requires specific color-states to access all chests

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Specialized room layout distinct from both action zone and palace; countdown display prominent on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced platforming challenge; player must complete required number of jumps; loss of all Mighty Coins upon exit; timer reset to 60 seconds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jack renders in blue color; orange treasure chests become accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited access mode; player can only open orange chests; encourages exploration for color variants

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jack renders in orange color; all treasure chests appear accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unrestricted access; player can open any chest by touching from side; maximum resource availability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jack renders in green color; on-screen enemies visibly transform into coins
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporary invulnerability window; 5-second duration; enemies neutralized; bonus collection opportunity
Urgency increased; player must complete level quickly or fail; temporal pressure on decision-making

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Automatic transition to Torture Room layout when thresholds exceeded (>9 Mighty Coins or >99 seconds)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Punishment mechanic triggered by over-collection; forces jump challenge completion before level resumption; Mighty Coins forfeit

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of 16 levels split into Action Zone and Royal Palace room; visual and mechanical contrast tells story of hero progressing from demon's outer domain (pyramid action zone) to inner sanctum (palace room) to rescue royal family
16-level pyramid structure physically represents descent into demon Belzebut's lair; increasingly complex/dangerous environment signals deeper penetration into hostile territory
Hidden vs. visible chests signal exploration reward; color-lock requirements (blue/orange Mighty Coins) suggest fortress security layers protecting royal family
Sphinx symbol within treasure chests unlocks secret passages; mythological iconography suggests ancient magical security protecting escape routes and shortcuts
Automatic penalty room triggered by greed tells meta-narrative: demon's curse punishes excessive ambition; mechanical consequence reinforces thematic balance (can't be too powerful)
Jack's visual color change via Mighty Coins signals power-up acquisition; blue/orange/green states represent different capability tiers, visually communicating progression within levels

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Reviews note 'almost random' behavior; players must anticipate movement to navigate safely; no consistent patrol patterns reduce predictability
Sprite graphics exist in ROM; were likely intended for slave rooms where royal family members are held; suggests planned stationary or patrol-based enemy variety
Standard platforming movement; restricted to accessible treasure chests; vulnerable to enemy collision
Can open orange treasure chests only; encourages hunting for orange/green coins; restricted resource access forces exploration
5-second window where all on-screen enemies transform to coins; enemies cease threat; enables high-risk collection strategies
Convert to collectible coins; threat neutralized; provides bonus resource window
Pressures player to complete zones quickly; Mighty Drink pickup extends duration; timer reset in Torture Room penalty

notes: Mighty Bomb Jack demonstrates world-state communication through dual-environment design (Action Zone vs. Royal Palace) and resource-gating via color-state mechanics. Danger signals rely heavily on enemy presence and temporal pressure (timer) rather than explicit visual markers. Safety signals are color-coded (blue/orange/green Mighty Coins) and provide both mechanical and visual feedback. The Torture Room serves as a unique penalty state that reinforces thematic balance through visual/mechanical consequence. The game's communication style is implicit and rewards player learning; enemy behavior is noted as unpredictable, requiring anticipation rather than pattern recognition. Limited source material on detailed enemy sprite design and environmental visual techniques; TCRF notes unused graphics suggest planned but removed enemy variety and UI elements (compass map).
